technologies.name: ICSharpCode
technologies.description:
​ICSharpCode is a collective of developers renowned for creating open-source .NET tools and libraries. Their projects aim to enhance developer productivity and provide robust solutions for various programming tasks. Notable contributions include:​

SharpZipLib: A versatile library designed for handling various compression formats such as ZIP, GZIP, and TAR within .NET applications. It offers comprehensive APIs for compressing and decompressing data streams and files. ​

AvalonEdit: A WPF-based text editor component that serves as the foundation for the SharpDevelop IDE's code editor. AvalonEdit is tailored for syntax highlighting and code editing, providing a customizable and efficient text editing experience.

ILSpy: A leading .NET assembly browser and decompiler that allows developers to inspect and analyze compiled .NET assemblies. ILSpy is widely recognized for its efficiency and user-friendly interface. ​

technologies.name: xUnit.net
technologies.description: xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for the .NET Framework. Written by the original inventor of NUnit v2, xUnit.net is the latest technology for unit testing C#, F#, VB.NET and other .NET languages. xUnit.net works with command line tools, Visual Studio, Visual Studio Code, ReSharper/Rider, CodeRush, and NCrunch. It is part of the .NET Foundation, and operates under their code of conduct.

technologies.name: .NET System Text

technologies.name: Novell.Directory.Ldap
technologies.description:
LDAP Libraries for C# enables you to write applications that access, manage, and update information stored in Novell eDirectory™ or other LDAP-aware directories.
These classes are based on the IETF LDAP Java Application Program Interface.

technologies.name: Apache log4net
technologies.description: The Apache log4net library is a tool to help the programmer output log statements to a variety of output targets. log4net is a port of the excellent Apache log4j™ framework to the Microsoft® .NET runtime. We have kept the framework similar in spirit to the original log4j while taking advantage of new features in the .NET runtime. For more information on log4net see the features document.
